#
# LLMS.TXT - AI LANGUAGE MODEL INSTRUCTIONS
#
# PRIMARY FUNCTION:
# This file provides structured information about your website specifically for AI language models.
# It helps LLMs understand your site's purpose, content, and context when answering questions about it.
#
# SECTION IMPORTANCE:
#
# 1. Core Information (CRITICAL)
#    - Essential facts: name, description, type, URL
#    - Helps LLMs identify and describe your platform accurately
#    - Should be concise but comprehensive
#
# 2. Key Features (HIGH IMPORTANCE)
#    - Main value propositions and capabilities
#    - Helps LLMs explain what users can do on your platform
#    - Useful for recommendation and comparison queries
#
# 3. Important Pages (HIGH IMPORTANCE)
#    - Direct links to key sections
#    - Helps LLMs provide specific navigation guidance
#    - Should include primary user journeys
#
# 4. Contact Information (MEDIUM IMPORTANCE)
#    - Social media and communication channels
#    - Enables LLMs to direct users to support/community
#    - Builds credibility and trust signals
#
# 5. Product Details (HIGH IMPORTANCE)
#    - Specific information about your main offering
#    - Helps LLMs provide accurate details about the product
#    - Should include key facts users frequently ask about
#
# BEST PRACTICES:
# - Keep information current and accurate
# - Use clear, descriptive language
# - Include quantifiable details (features, system requirements)
# - Provide absolute URLs
# - Update when major features/details change
# - Consider what users would most commonly ask about
#

# Core Information - Essential facts about the platform
name: Langflow Documentation
description: Official documentation for Langflow, a low-code platform for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows with a visual builder, API, and MCP server.
type: Technical Documentation - AI Development Platform
url: https://docs.langflow.org

# Key Features - Main value propositions and platform capabilities
- Visual flow builder to design AI workflows with drag-and-drop
- Get started guides: installation, quickstart, and tutorials (RAG, files, agents, MCP)
- Flows: build, run via API or webhooks, test in playground, import/export
- Agents and tools; Model Context Protocol (MCP) client and server
- Develop: API keys, custom dependencies, global variables, storage, memory, observability (LangSmith, LangFuse, etc.)
- Deploy: public server, Nginx/SSL, Docker, Kubernetes, GCP, Hugging Face Spaces, Railway, Render; security
- Components reference: inputs/outputs, prompts, models, retrievers, and more
- API reference and contribution guidelines

# Important Pages - Primary navigation destinations for users
- Home / About: https://docs.langflow.org/Get-Started/about-langflow
- Install Langflow: https://docs.langflow.org/Get-Started/get-started-installation
- Quickstart: https://docs.langflow.org/Get-Started/get-started-quickstart
- Use the visual editor: https://docs.langflow.org/Flows/concepts-overview
- Trigger flows with the API: https://docs.langflow.org/Flows/concepts-publish
- Agents: https://docs.langflow.org/Agents/agents
- MCP client and server: https://docs.langflow.org/Agents/mcp-client
- Deployment overview: https://docs.langflow.org/Deployment/deployment-overview
- Security: https://docs.langflow.org/Deployment/security
- Release notes: https://docs.langflow.org/Support/release-notes

# Contact Information - Community and support channels
- GitHub (source): https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow
- Main website: https://langflow.org
- Desktop app: https://langflow.org/desktop
- Discord: https://discord.com/invite/EqksyE2EX9
- Twitter/X: https://x.com/langflow_ai
- YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@Langflow

# Product Details - Specific information about Langflow
- Platform: Open-source Python application; also available as Langflow Desktop (Windows, macOS)
- License: MIT
- Requirements: Python 3.10–3.13; uv or pip for install; Docker optional
- Run locally: uv run langflow run (default http://127.0.0.1:7860)
- Use cases: RAG apps, multi-agent workflows, MCP tools, API-backed AI services
- Target users: Developers, data scientists, AI engineers, teams building LLM applications
